quoted_text: Manual theme preference toggle in settings
meaning_a: "appearance setting" — the base spec's term for the stored light/dark choice
meaning_b: "theme preference" — the proposal's coinage, inherited by the delta spec and tasks
decision_needed: Adopt "appearance setting" throughout the change, or make the rename explicit in the delta so the base term is updated rather than shadowed.

meaning_a: "Setting Persistence" — the base spec's requirement header
meaning_b: "Preference Persistence" — the delta's reworded header, which OpenSpec's exact header match treats as a different requirement at archive time
decision_needed: Retitle the MODIFIED header to exactly "Setting Persistence"; rewording a requirement title is a separate, explicit step.

quoted_text: A single DisplayModeStore owns the current selection
meaning_a: DisplayModeStore holds the appearance setting / theme preference itself
meaning_b: DisplayModeStore is a separate display-state object, with preference storage handled elsewhere (profile-service mirror)
decision_needed: Add one bridge sentence to the design stating what DisplayModeStore holds, in the spec's vocabulary.

quoted_text: Wire the toggle through the preference bridge
meaning_a: none found — nearest names: DisplayModeStore, profile-service mirror, theme preference
decision_needed: Replace "the preference bridge" with the established name intended, or introduce and define it in design.md before tasks reference it.
